name: Maca Lepidium meyenii
summary: Maca (Lepidium meyenii) is traditionally used for energy and libido support, with early evidence exploring effects on mood and hormonal balance through its macamide and glucosinolate compounds.
description: Maca (Lepidium meyenii) is traditionally used for energy and libido support, with early evidence exploring effects on mood and hormonal balance through its macamide and glucosinolate compounds.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated; GI upset possible

claimMap.claim: v9.4: Maca has limited human evidence for sexual desire, but hormone/testosterone claims should be avoided.
claimMap.notes: v9.4: Maca has limited human evidence for sexual desire, but hormone/testosterone claims should be avoided.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 1.5–3 g/day gelatinized or powdered maca used in libido trials

dosage: Gelatinized powder: 1.5-3 g/day; extracts vary; limited standardization
typical_dosage: Gelatinized powder: 1.5-3 g/day; extracts vary; limited standardization
